questions.text: What does the Surah say about the nature of the Quran in its opening?
questions.answer: All praise is for Allah who sent down the Book to His servant and has not made therein any deviance; it is a straight Book.

questions.text: What is the warning given to those who say 'Allah has taken a son'?
questions.answer: They have no knowledge of it, nor did their fathers; it is a monstrous word that comes out of their mouths—they speak nothing but a lie.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the purpose of the adornments on the earth?
questions.answer: He has made whatever is on the earth an adornment for it to test mankind as to which of them is best in deed.

questions.text: Why did the young men flee to the cave?
questions.answer: To protect their faith (Iman) from a tyrannical, pagan king and to seek mercy and guidance from their Lord.

questions.text: What was the prayer of the young men when they entered the cave?
questions.answer: 'Our Lord, grant us from Yourself mercy and prepare for us from our affair right guidance.'

questions.text: How did Allah protect the young men from the sun while they were in the cave?
questions.answer: The sun would pass to the right of their cave when rising and turn away from them to the left when setting, while they lay in the open space within.

questions.text: What would have happened to a person who looked at the sleeping young men in the cave?
questions.answer: They would have been filled with terror and would have turned and fled.

questions.text: What was the role of the dog in the story of the People of the Cave?
questions.answer: He was stretching his forelegs at the entrance, guarding the young men.

questions.text: What did the young men think about the duration of their sleep when they woke up?
questions.answer: Some said they had stayed a day or part of a day.

questions.text: What was the instruction given to the one who went into the city to buy food?
questions.answer: To find the purest food and to be cautious and subtle so that no one would notice them.

questions.text: Why were the young men afraid of being discovered by the people of the city?
questions.answer: Because the people would either stone them to death or force them to return to their pagan religion, and then they would never succeed.

questions.text: What did the people of the city decide to do once the story of the young men was known?
questions.answer: They disputed among themselves; eventually, those who prevailed decided to build a place of worship (Masjid) over them.

questions.text: What does Allah say about the dispute over the number of the People of the Cave?
questions.answer: People make guesses without knowledge; Allah knows best their number, and only a few people know the truth.

questions.text: What phrase must a believer say when planning for the future?
questions.answer: In Shaa Allah (If Allah wills).

questions.text: How long did the young men remain in their cave according to this Surah?
questions.answer: Three hundred years and nine more (309 years).

questions.text: With whom should the Prophet remain patient and keep company?
questions.answer: With those who call upon their Lord morning and afternoon, seeking His countenance, and he should not let his eyes pass beyond them for worldly adornment.

questions.text: What is the description of the 'Fire' prepared for the wrongdoers?
questions.answer: Its walls will surround them; if they ask for relief, they will be given water like murky oil, which scalds their faces.

questions.text: What are the rewards for the believers in Paradise as mentioned here?
questions.answer: Gardens of perpetual residence (Adn), ornaments of gold, green garments of fine silk and brocade, reclining on adorned couches.

questions.text: In the story of the Two Gardens, what were the two men's attitudes?
questions.answer: One was arrogant and ungrateful, boasting of his wealth and children; the other was a humble believer who reminded him of Allah's power.

questions.text: What was the sin of the owner of the better garden?
questions.answer: Arrogance, ungratefulness to Allah, and doubting that his garden would ever perish or that the Hour would come.

questions.text: What did the believer say to the arrogant garden owner about his creation?
questions.answer: He asked: 'Do you disbelieve in Him who created you from dust, then from a sperm-drop, then proportioned you as a man?'

questions.text: What happened to the gardens of the arrogant man?
questions.answer: They were destroyed by a storm from the sky, leaving the vines collapsed and the owner in regret, saying, 'Oh, I wish I had not associated anyone with my Lord.'

questions.text: To what is the life of this world compared in this Surah?
questions.answer: To vegetation that flourishes after rain but then dries up, becomes chaff, and is scattered by the wind.

questions.text: What are 'the adornments of the life of this world'?
questions.answer: Wealth and children; but the enduring good deeds are better with your Lord.

questions.text: What will happen to the mountains on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: Allah will cause them to pass away, and the earth will be seen as a bare plain.

questions.text: What will the criminals say when they see the 'Book of Deeds'?
questions.answer: 'Oh, woe to us! What is this book that leaves nothing small or great except that it has enumerated it?'

questions.text: Why did Iblis refuse to prostrate to Adam according to this Surah?
questions.answer: He was from the Jinn and defiantly disobeyed the command of his Lord.

questions.text: What is the warning against taking Shaitan and his offspring as allies?
questions.answer: They are enemies to mankind; it is a wretched exchange for the wrongdoers.

questions.text: Does Allah take the misleaders as assistants in His creation?
questions.answer: No, He did not make them witnesses to the creation of the heavens and earth, nor even to their own creation.

questions.text: What happens when people are called to the guidance of their Lord but turn away?
questions.answer: Their hearts are veiled and their ears are made heavy so they cannot understand the truth.

questions.text: What was the destination Musa (AS) was determined to reach with his servant?
questions.answer: The junction of the two seas.

questions.text: What sign indicated that Musa (AS) had reached the right place?
questions.answer: Their fish, which they had forgotten, took its way into the sea miraculously.

questions.text: Who did Musa (AS) meet at the junction of the two seas?
questions.answer: A servant of Allah (Khidr) whom Allah had given mercy and knowledge from Himself.

questions.text: What was Musa's request to Khidr?
questions.answer: 'May I follow you on [the condition] that you teach me from what you have been taught of sound judgment?'

questions.text: What was Khidr's initial response to Musa's request?
questions.answer: He said, 'Indeed, you will never be able to have patience with me. And how can you have patience for that which you do not encompass in knowledge?'

questions.text: What was the condition Khidr set for Musa to follow him?
questions.answer: Musa was not to ask about anything until Khidr himself mentioned it.

questions.text: What was the first action Khidr took that Musa questioned?
questions.answer: He made a hole in the ship they were traveling in.

questions.text: What was the second action Khidr took that shocked Musa?
questions.answer: He killed a young boy they encountered.

questions.text: What was the third action Khidr took in a town that refused them food?
questions.answer: He repaired a wall that was about to collapse without asking for any payment.

questions.text: Why did Khidr damage the ship?
questions.answer: To make it defective so a tyrannical king, who was seizing every good ship by force, would leave it for its poor owners.

questions.text: Why did Khidr kill the boy?
questions.answer: His parents were believers, and it was feared he would grow up to be a disbeliever and oppress them; Allah intended to replace him with a better child.

questions.text: Why did Khidr repair the wall?
questions.answer: It belonged to two orphan boys in the city, and a treasure for them was hidden beneath it; Allah wanted it preserved until they reached maturity.

questions.text: Did Khidr do these things of his own accord?
questions.answer: No, he said, 'I did it not of my own accord,' implying it was by divine command.

questions.text: Who was Dhul-Qarnayn?
questions.answer: A powerful, righteous king and traveler who was given means and authority by Allah to establish justice.

questions.text: What did Dhul-Qarnayn see when he reached the setting place of the sun?
questions.answer: He found it setting in a spring of dark mud and found a people near it.

questions.text: What was Dhul-Qarnayn's policy regarding the people he ruled?
questions.answer: To punish the wrongdoers and reward the believers with good.

questions.text: What did Dhul-Qarnayn find when he reached the rising place of the sun?
questions.answer: A people for whom Allah had provided no shelter from the sun.

questions.text: What problem did the people living between the two mountains have?
questions.answer: They were being harassed and corrupted by Gog and Magog (Ya'juj and Ma'juj).

questions.text: What did Dhul-Qarnayn build to protect these people?
questions.answer: A massive barrier/wall made of iron blocks and molten copper.

questions.text: When will the barrier of Gog and Magog be destroyed?
questions.answer: When the promise of Allah comes, He will level it to the ground.

questions.text: Who are the 'greatest losers' in terms of their deeds?
questions.answer: Those whose effort is lost in worldly life while they think they are doing well.

questions.text: What is the reward for those who believe and do righteous deeds?
questions.answer: They will have the Gardens of Firdaws (Paradise) as a lodging, staying there forever.

questions.text: What would happen if the sea were ink for the words of Allah?
questions.answer: The sea would be exhausted before the words of my Lord were finished, even if another sea were brought to supplement it.

questions.text: What is the final message of the Surah regarding the Prophet's nature and the core duty of a believer?
questions.answer: The Prophet is but a man like others, but receives revelation that your God is One God; so whoever hopes for the meeting with his Lord should do righteous deeds and not associate anyone in worship.

questions.text: What is the 'special virtue' associated with the first ten verses of Surah Al-Kahf?
questions.answer: Protection from the trial of the Dajjal (Antichrist).

questions.text: What is the meaning of the name 'Al-Kahf'?
questions.answer: The Cave.

questions.text: Who is the 'servant' mentioned in the opening ayah?
questions.answer: Prophet Muhammad (SAW).

questions.text: What is the 'straight' attribute of the Quran?
questions.answer: That it has no contradictions or errors and leads directly to the truth.

questions.text: What is the 'monstrous word' mentioned early in the Surah?
questions.answer: The claim that Allah has taken a son.

questions.text: What is the 'Al-Raqim' mentioned alongside the Cave?
questions.answer: It is believed to be the tablet or inscription that contained the names of the People of the Cave.

questions.text: How did the young men describe their Lord?
questions.answer: As the Lord of the heavens and the earth.

questions.text: What is the 'Open Space' (Fajwah) in the cave?
questions.answer: The wide area where the young men slept, allowing air and protection.

questions.text: What is the 'guidance' Allah gives to those who believe?
questions.answer: He increases them in guidance and makes their hearts firm.

questions.text: What is the 'purest food' (Azka Ta'aman)?
questions.answer: Food that is Halal and earned through lawful means.

questions.text: What is the 'dispute' regarding the number of sleepers a sign of?
questions.answer: Human tendency to argue about unimportant details instead of focusing on the lesson.

questions.text: What is the 'mercy' of the Lord mentioned in the context of the cave?
questions.answer: Allah's protection and provision for the young men during their long sleep.

questions.text: What is the 'truth' from the Lord mentioned in the middle of the Surah?
questions.answer: The message of the Quran which people are free to believe or reject, but with consequences.

questions.text: What is the 'murky oil' drink in Hell?
questions.answer: A boiling, disgusting liquid that causes extreme pain.

questions.text: What is the 'brocade' (Istabraq) mentioned in Paradise?
questions.answer: A type of fine, thick silk clothing for the believers.

questions.text: What is the 'arrogance' of the garden owner?
questions.answer: Thinking his wealth was his own doing and would last forever.

questions.text: What is the 'enduring good deeds' (Al-Baqiyat al-Salihat)?
questions.answer: Righteous actions and speech (like Tasbih) that remain in one's record forever.

questions.text: What is the 'junction of the two seas'?
questions.answer: The specific place where Musa was to meet Khidr.

questions.text: What is the 'mercy from Us' given to Khidr?
questions.answer: Prophethood or a special spiritual rank and knowledge.

questions.text: What is the 'inner meaning' (Ta'wil) Khidr explained to Musa?
questions.answer: The divine wisdom behind actions that appear negative on the surface.

questions.text: What is the 'means' (Sababa) given to Dhul-Qarnayn?
questions.answer: The resources, knowledge, and power to achieve his goals.

questions.text: What is the 'molten copper' (Qitr) used for?
questions.answer: To seal the iron blocks and make the barrier impenetrable.

questions.text: What is the 'promise of my Lord' regarding the barrier?
questions.answer: Its eventual destruction as a sign of the Day of Judgment.

questions.text: What is 'Firdaws'?
questions.answer: The highest level of Paradise.

questions.text: What is the 'ink' for the words of Allah symbolic of?
questions.answer: The infinite nature of Allah's knowledge and wisdom.

questions.text: What is the 'meeting with the Lord'?
questions.answer: The standing for judgment and the reward of seeing Allah.
